publication.note: This open build measures impact with the PDH SDG 11.5.1 affected-persons series (country-year) linked to IBTrACS storms by track proximity. The PDH SDG dataset licence is confirmed as open; every source is open data. The DesInventar cross-check export is archived locally only and is not redistributed.

caveats: Affected counts are annual and cover every disaster in that year, not the named cyclone alone.
A country-year is linked to a storm only if a track passed within 500 km of its centroid; some reported tolls (floods, droughts) have no nearby cyclone.
Resolution is country-year, so one point is the strongest storm of the year, not a single landfall.
Only 15 of 21 territories report to this series; Guam, CNMI, Niue and others are absent.
Reported impact depends on what institutions were able to record; missing does not mean zero.

sources.name: International Best Track Archive for Climate Stewardship
sources.subset: South Pacific and Western Pacific basin files
sources.usedFor: storm tracks, peak wind, category, gale-force wind radii and annual storm trends

sources.name: World Population Prospects
sources.subset: 21 Pacific countries and territories, annual population 1950–2023
sources.usedFor: turning reported affected people into a share of national population

sources.name: Climate Change indicators: mean sea-surface temperature anomalies
sources.subset: SST_ANOM for 21 Pacific island countries and territories
sources.usedFor: the opening warming-stripes and annual anomaly line

sources.name: Natural Earth land boundaries via world-atlas
SDG 11.5.1: number of directly affected persons attributed to disasters
sources.provider: Pacific Community (SPC) / Pacific Data Hub; compiled from UNDRR Sendai Framework Monitor
sources.subset: VC_DSR_AFFCT for the reporting Pacific island countries and territories
sources.usedFor: the open annual affected-persons outcome (share of population affected)

transformations.title: Average the ocean context
transformations.summary: Group SST_ANOM by year and take an unweighted mean across the 21 available Pacific places.

transformations.title: Summarise each cyclone season
transformations.summary: Keep tropical storms at or above 34 kt, then count storms and average their lifetime peak wind by season.

transformations.title: Prepare the map
transformations.summary: Keep six-hour main-track positions, normalise longitudes around the dateline and draw them over the 110m land boundary.

transformations.title: Link storms to countries from open tracks
transformations.summary: For each country and year, keep the strongest IBTrACS storm whose track passed within 500 km of the country centroid.

transformations.title: Put countries on a comparable scale
transformations.summary: Divide the year's reported affected people by that country's population; use the 2023 population for later years.

transformations.title: Build a wind-only baseline
transformations.summary: Fit a simple straight line in log space, then measure how far every complete country-year sits above or below it.

sst.aggregation: unweighted arithmetic mean of every available PICT anomaly in each year

stormTrend.aggregation: one row per storm and season; count storms and average each storm's basin-lifetime peak wind
stormTrend.windRule: USA_WIND, with WMO_WIND only where USA_WIND is missing in the full-basin trend

map.trackSampling: main or provisional track at 6-hour synoptic times; coordinates rounded to 0.01°

heta.method: median of the largest reported USA_R34 quadrant at each valid track time

windFields.label: 34 kt wind field at 150 kt peak

join.rule: IBTrACS track point within 500 km of the country centroid in the same calendar year; strongest such storm kept

impact.outcomeSource: PDH VC_DSR_AFFCT (SDG 11.5.1): people affected by all disasters per year
impact.windRule: basin-lifetime maximum USA_WIND (kt) of the strongest storm within 500 km of the centroid that year

model.residual: observed log10 impact minus the value predicted by the wind-only OLS line
model.band: 25th, 50th and 75th percentiles within six equally populated intensity bins

license_note: This open build measures impact with the PDH SDG 11.5.1 affected-persons series (country-year) linked to IBTrACS storms by track proximity. The PDH SDG dataset licence is confirmed as open; every source is open data. The DesInventar cross-check export is archived locally only and is not redistributed.
